title: Ada Dungeon Walk — Concrete Intro Curriculum

projects.name: Project 1 — Dungeon Walk
projects.focus: Introductory Ada through one cumulative project
projects.goal: Build the supplied terminal ray-caster one understandable milestone at a time, beginning with the exact Alire initialization workflow.
projects.final_product: A playable terminal Doom-style ray-caster, followed by an introductory package refactor that preserves behavior.

chapters.name: Create the Alire Project Correctly
chapters.concept: Alire workspace creation and project layout
chapters.deliverable: Create one long-lived dungeon_walk binary crate in the directory where all Intro Ada quests will be completed.

chapters.name: Build, Run, and Edit the Generated Program
chapters.concept: Compilation cycle and Ada procedure structure
chapters.deliverable: Understand the generated main procedure and complete the edit-build-run cycle.

chapters.name: Import Text I/O and Read Ada Declarations
chapters.concept: with clauses, use clauses, declarations, and begin/end
chapters.deliverable: Understand the basic regions of an Ada procedure and why Text_IO is visible.

chapters.name: Add Screen Settings and Player State
chapters.concept: constants, variables, Positive, Float, Boolean, and Character
chapters.deliverable: Declare immutable configuration separately from mutable game state.

chapters.name: Create the Main Input Loop
chapters.concept: while loops, immediate character input, and case statements
chapters.deliverable: Keep the program running, read one key at a time, and exit cleanly with Q.

chapters.name: Model the Ship Map with a Subtype and Array
chapters.concept: String constraints, subtypes, arrays, and multidimensional indexing
chapters.deliverable: Represent a fixed 16 by 16 map so incorrect row lengths are rejected.

chapters.name: Print the Top-Down Map with a Procedure
chapters.concept: procedures, for loops, array attributes, and parameters
chapters.deliverable: Write a reusable procedure that displays every map row.

chapters.name: Create an Off-Screen Character Buffer
chapters.concept: arrays of constrained strings and mutable aggregate state
chapters.deliverable: Store a complete 80 by 24 terminal image before printing it.

chapters.name: Fill Ceiling and Floor with Nested Loops
chapters.concept: nested iteration, conditions, and array assignment
chapters.deliverable: Initialize every frame with blank ceiling and dotted floor.

chapters.name: Write Is_Wall Safely
chapters.concept: functions, return values, short-circuit Boolean logic, conversion, and bounds
chapters.deliverable: Convert world coordinates to map indexes without ever indexing outside the map.

chapters.name: Use Floating-Point Player Coordinates
chapters.concept: Float state, assignments, and tile versus world coordinates
chapters.deliverable: Allow the player to occupy positions between map tile centers.

chapters.name: Turn with Radians and Compute Direction
chapters.concept: Ada.Numerics, elementary functions, radians, Sin, and Cos
chapters.deliverable: Represent facing direction with an angle and convert it into X/Y direction components.

chapters.name: Move Forward, Backward, and Slide Along Walls
chapters.concept: procedure parameters, local constants, collision checks, and state mutation
chapters.deliverable: Use one movement procedure for both forward and backward travel while preventing entry into walls.

chapters.name: Choose a Directional Minimap Symbol
chapters.concept: functions without parameters, local constants, abs, nested conditions
chapters.deliverable: Return >, <, v, or ^ based on the player's strongest direction component.

chapters.name: Draw the Map into the Screen Buffer
chapters.concept: coordinate translation, nested loops, overlays, and constant local indexes
chapters.deliverable: Copy the hidden map into the upper-right of Screen and overlay the player arrow.

chapters.name: Cast One Ray Until It Reaches a Wall
chapters.concept: incremental search loops, direction vectors, and exit conditions
chapters.deliverable: Trace one ray from the player through the map and report the wall distance.

chapters.name: Cast a Different Ray for Every Screen Column
chapters.concept: linear interpolation, field of view, loop indexes, and Float conversion
chapters.deliverable: Sweep rays from the left edge of the field of view to the right edge.

chapters.name: Project Wall Distance into Vertical Slices
chapters.concept: perspective projection, numeric conversion, clamping, and ranges
chapters.deliverable: Turn each ray distance into a wall slice between Ceiling_Row and Floor_Row.

chapters.name: Shade Walls and Correct Fish-Eye Distortion
chapters.concept: piecewise functions and perpendicular-distance correction
chapters.deliverable: Use distance-dependent symbols and keep flat walls visually flat.

chapters.name: Assemble the Render Pipeline
chapters.concept: procedure composition, deterministic ordering, and status overlays
chapters.deliverable: Produce one complete frame in a predictable sequence.

chapters.name: Complete and Verify the Monolithic Ray-Caster
chapters.concept: integration, behavior verification, and controlled shutdown
chapters.deliverable: Match the supplied final Dungeon Walk behavior in one source file.

chapters.name: Refactor the Working Program into Introductory Packages
chapters.concept: package specifications, package bodies, encapsulation, and dependency boundaries
chapters.deliverable: Preserve behavior while separating maps, player movement, rendering, and the main loop.

quests.title: 1.1 — Understand the Next Milestone
quests.lesson: Alire manages the Ada crate, compiler, dependencies, and build profiles. The --bin option creates an executable crate. The --in-place option initializes the current directory instead of creating another nested dungeon_walk directory.
quests.prompt: Read the lesson, inspect the listed files, and state what the program will be able to do after completing milestone 1: Create the Alire Project Correctly.
instructions: Do not jump ahead to later ray-casting code; focus only on Alire workspace creation and project layout.
Locate each primary file or note that it will be created during this milestone.
Describe how this milestone contributes to the final playable Dungeon Walk program.
quests.code_to_add:
mkdir -p ~/Documents/src/projects/ada-mastery/dungeon_walk
cd ~/Documents/src/projects/ada-mastery/dungeon_walk
alr init --bin --in-place dungeon_walk
acceptance: The work advances this exact milestone: Create one long-lived dungeon_walk binary crate in the directory where all Intro Ada quests will be completed.
The project still uses the same dungeon_walk Alire workspace.
The result is explained in the learner's own words rather than only copied.
rubric: The answer names the immediate behavior being added.
The answer connects that behavior to Alire workspace creation and project layout.
The answer distinguishes this milestone from later work.
quests.answer_guide: After this milestone, create one long-lived dungeon_walk binary crate in the directory where all Intro Ada quests will be completed. The key Ada topic is Alire workspace creation and project layout.
quests.hint: Describe only the next visible or structural improvement, not the entire final game.
quests.alire_commands:
mkdir -p ~/Documents/src/projects/ada-mastery/dungeon_walk
cd ~/Documents/src/projects/ada-mastery/dungeon_walk
alr init --bin --in-place dungeon_walk
find . -maxdepth 2 -type f -print
